author: EdgyBruh
msg:
With the new IP/subnet filtering, it's no longer enough to just run devices on different ISPs. If the public IP or subnet is the same, those devices are still grouped as a cluster and rewards will be shit.
From what I've seen, the key factor now is having unique public IPs, not just different providers. No more than 5 -10 devices per unique public IP to stay within safe limits.
So optimization now (from what I can see in my setup) is less about adding more SIMs or ISPs, and more about managing IP distribution and density per IP

author: Dennis

author: Mr Roundry
msg:
Nothing has changed in terms of boundaries or recommended devices - up to 20 per location, same as always. 

What changed is that thousands of ULOs were abusing the system - VPNs, synthetic networking, emulators, synthetic devices and more.

That abuse was damaging the network’s value and degrading the quality of data being delivered to telemetry customers. 

Lower quality can mean lower demand - and demand is what drives your rewards. The cleanup removed those bad actors, but the network is still recalibrating after that. Give it time.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
